exampleSentences.japanese: このポスターはかっこいいです。
exampleSentences.english: This poster is cool.

exampleSentences.japanese: へやにえいがのポスターをはりました。
exampleSentences.english: I put up a movie poster in my room.

exampleSentences.japanese: むすこはアニメがだいすきですから、へやにたくさんポスターがあります。
exampleSentences.english: My son loves anime, so there are many posters in his room.

exampleSentences.japanese: あのはいゆうはゆうめいです。
exampleSentences.english: That actor is famous.

exampleSentences.japanese: このえいがのはいゆうはだれですか。
exampleSentences.english: Who is the actor in this movie?

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のすきなはいゆうは日本のドラマによくでますから、まいしゅう見ています。
exampleSentences.english: My favorite actor often appears in Japanese dramas, so I watch them every week.

updatedWords.english: does not see, do not watch, does not look at

exampleSentences.japanese: テレビを見ません。
exampleSentences.english: I don't watch TV.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はホラーえいがを見ません。
exampleSentences.english: I don't watch horror movies.

exampleSentences.japanese: 父はいそがしいですから、テレビをあまり見ませんが、ニュースだけは見ます。
exampleSentences.english: My father is busy, so he doesn't watch much TV, but he only watches the news.

exampleSentences.japanese: カードゲームがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like card games.

exampleSentences.japanese: しゅうまつ、かぞくとカードゲームをしませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to play card games with the family this weekend?

exampleSentences.japanese: 雨の日はそとにでかけませんから、こどもといっしょにカードゲームをたのしみます。
exampleSentences.english: On rainy days, we don't go outside, so I enjoy card games with the children.

updatedWords.english: do not, will not, does not

exampleSentences.japanese: 今日はうんどうしません。
exampleSentences.english: I won't exercise today.

exampleSentences.japanese: 日曜日はしごとをしません。
exampleSentences.english: I don't work on Sundays.

exampleSentences.japanese: つかれていますから、今日はそうじもせんたくもしません。
exampleSentences.english: I'm tired, so I won't do cleaning or laundry today.

exampleSentences.japanese: パズルがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like puzzles.

exampleSentences.japanese: このパズルはむずかしいですが、おもしろいです。
exampleSentences.english: This puzzle is difficult, but interesting.

exampleSentences.japanese: おばあさんはまいにちパズルをしますから、あたまがとてもいいです。
exampleSentences.english: My grandmother does puzzles every day, so she's very smart.

exampleSentences.japanese: ラジオをききません。
exampleSentences.english: I don't listen to the radio.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はロックをあまりききません。
exampleSentences.english: I don't really listen to rock music.

exampleSentences.japanese: ジャズはあまりききませんが、Jポップとポップスはよくききます。
exampleSentences.english: I don't really listen to jazz, but I often listen to J-pop and pop music.

exampleSentences.japanese: ポップスがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like pop music.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はポップスをよくききます。
exampleSentences.english: I often listen to pop music.

exampleSentences.japanese: むすめはKポップとポップスがだいすきですから、まいにちきいています。
exampleSentences.english: My daughter loves K-pop and pop music, so she listens to them every day.

exampleSentences.japanese: ノンフィクションを読みます。
exampleSentences.english: I read non-fiction.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はフィクションよりノンフィクションがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like non-fiction more than fiction.

exampleSentences.japanese: 父はれきしのノンフィクションをよく読みますから、本だながいっぱいです。
exampleSentences.english: My father often reads history non-fiction, so his bookshelf is full.

exampleSentences.japanese: いい話ですね。
exampleSentences.english: That's a nice story.

exampleSentences.japanese: ともだちとの話はいつもたのしいです。
exampleSentences.english: Conversations with my friends are always fun.

exampleSentences.japanese: あにはおもしろい話をたくさん知っていますから、いっしょにいるとたのしいです。
exampleSentences.english: My older brother knows many interesting stories, so it's fun to be with him.

exampleSentences.japanese: チョコミントがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like mint chocolate chip.

exampleSentences.japanese: チョコミントのアイスクリームをください。
exampleSentences.english: Mint chocolate chip ice cream, please.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はチョコミントがだいすきですが、かれしはあまりすきじゃないです。
exampleSentences.english: I love mint chocolate chip, but my boyfriend doesn't really like it.

exampleSentences.japanese: バニラのアイスクリームがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like vanilla ice cream.

exampleSentences.japanese: すきなあじは何ですか。バニラです。
exampleSentences.english: What's your favorite flavor? Vanilla.

exampleSentences.japanese: こどもはバニラとチョコレートのアイスクリームがだいすきですから、よく買います。
exampleSentences.english: The children love vanilla and chocolate ice cream, so I often buy them.

exampleSentences.japanese: このはなはきいろいです。
exampleSentences.english: This flower is yellow.

exampleSentences.japanese: むすめはきいろいドレスがほしいです。
exampleSentences.english: My daughter wants a yellow dress.

exampleSentences.japanese: はるになると、にわにきいろいはながたくさんさきますから、とてもきれいです。
exampleSentences.english: When spring comes, many yellow flowers bloom in the garden, so it's very beautiful.

exampleSentences.japanese: ショーを見ます。
exampleSentences.english: I'll watch the show.

exampleSentences.japanese: ゆうえんちでショーを見ませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to watch a show at the theme park?

exampleSentences.japanese: このゆうえんちのショーはとてもにんきですから、はやく行きましょう。
exampleSentences.english: The show at this theme park is very popular, so let's go early.

exampleSentences.japanese: えいがを見ましょうか。
exampleSentences.english: Should we watch a movie?

exampleSentences.japanese: 今夜、いっしょにテレビを見ましょうか。
exampleSentences.english: Should we watch TV together tonight?

exampleSentences.japanese: 雨ですから、うちでえいがを見ましょうか。ポップコーンも買いましょう。
exampleSentences.english: It's raining, so should we watch a movie at home? Let's also buy popcorn.

exampleSentences.japanese: ゴーカートにのりたいです。
exampleSentences.english: I want to ride a go-kart.

exampleSentences.japanese: ゆうえんちでゴーカートにのりませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to ride go-karts at the theme park?

exampleSentences.japanese: むすこはゴーカートがだいすきですから、ゆうえんちに行くといつものります。
exampleSentences.english: My son loves go-karts, so he always rides them when we go to the theme park.

exampleSentences.japanese: すきな食べ物は何ですか。
exampleSentences.english: What is your favorite food?

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のすきないろはあおです。
exampleSentences.english: My favorite color is blue.

exampleSentences.japanese: すきなアーティストのコンサートがありますから、来月東京に行きます。
exampleSentences.english: There's a concert by my favorite artist, so I'm going to Tokyo next month.

exampleSentences.japanese: このアトラクションはおもしろいです。
exampleSentences.english: This attraction is fun.

exampleSentences.japanese: すきなアトラクションは何ですか。
exampleSentences.english: What's your favorite attraction?

exampleSentences.japanese: このゆうえんちにはアトラクションがたくさんありますが、ジェットコースターがいちばんにんきです。
exampleSentences.english: This theme park has many attractions, but the rollercoaster is the most popular.

exampleSentences.japanese: このえいがはこわいです。
exampleSentences.english: This movie is scary.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はこわいえいがを見ません。
exampleSentences.english: I don't watch scary movies.

exampleSentences.japanese: ジェットコースターはこわいですが、かのじょはだいすきです。
exampleSentences.english: Rollercoasters are scary, but my girlfriend loves them.

exampleSentences.japanese: これは三千円です。
exampleSentences.english: This is 3,000 yen.

exampleSentences.japanese: にゅうじょうりょうは三千円です。
exampleSentences.english: The admission fee is 3,000 yen.

exampleSentences.japanese: このはくぶつかんのにゅうじょうりょうは三千円ですが、がくせいは千円です。
exampleSentences.english: The admission fee for this museum is 3,000 yen, but it's 1,000 yen for students.

exampleSentences.japanese: ゆうえんちに行きます。
exampleSentences.english: I'm going to a theme park.

exampleSentences.japanese: こんしゅうまつ、かぞくとゆうえんちに行きませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to go to a theme park with the family this weekend?

exampleSentences.japanese: こどものたんじょうびに、ゆうえんちに行ってジェットコースターにのりたいです。
exampleSentences.english: For my child's birthday, I want to go to a theme park and ride the rollercoaster.

exampleSentences.japanese: あのせんしゅはゆうめいです。
exampleSentences.english: That athlete is famous.

exampleSentences.japanese: このサッカーせんしゅはとてもはやいです。
exampleSentences.english: This soccer player is very fast.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のすきなやきゅうせんしゅは日本でとてもゆうめいですから、サインがほしいです。
exampleSentences.english: My favorite baseball player is very famous in Japan, so I want their autograph.

exampleSentences.japanese: かれはプロのせんしゅです。
exampleSentences.english: He is a professional athlete.

exampleSentences.japanese: あにはプロのしゃしんかです。
exampleSentences.english: My older brother is a professional photographer.

exampleSentences.japanese: プロのサッカーせんしゅになりたいですから、まいにちれんしゅうしています。
exampleSentences.english: I want to become a professional soccer player, so I practice every day.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のチームはつよいです。
exampleSentences.english: My team is strong.

exampleSentences.japanese: このチームには十人います。
exampleSentences.english: There are ten people on this team.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のすきなやきゅうチームはきょねんしあいにかちましたから、とてもうれしかったです。
exampleSentences.english: My favorite baseball team won the game last year, so I was very happy.

exampleSentences.japanese: ごぜん九時におきます。
exampleSentences.english: I wake up at 9 a.m.

exampleSentences.japanese: ミーティングはごぜん十時からです。
exampleSentences.english: The meeting is from 10 a.m.

exampleSentences.japanese: ごぜん六時におきて、シャワーをあびてから、あさごはんを食べます。
exampleSentences.english: I wake up at 6 a.m., take a shower, and then eat breakfast.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私は車をうんてんします。
exampleSentences.english: I drive a car.

exampleSentences.japanese: 父はまいにち車をうんてんしてしごとに行きます。
exampleSentences.english: My father drives to work every day.

exampleSentences.japanese: 来週、かぞくと車でほっかいどうまでうんてんしますから、ナビがひつようです。
exampleSentences.english: Next week, I'll drive to Hokkaido with my family, so I need a GPS.

exampleSentences.japanese: ナビをつかいます。
exampleSentences.english: I use GPS.

exampleSentences.japanese: 車にナビがありますから、べんりです。
exampleSentences.english: There's GPS in the car, so it's convenient.

exampleSentences.japanese: はじめてのところに行くとき、いつもナビをつかいますから、みちにまよいません。
exampleSentences.english: When I go to a place for the first time, I always use GPS, so I don't get lost.

exampleSentences.japanese: あねはほんやくかです。
exampleSentences.english: My older sister is a translator.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はほんやくかになりたいです。
exampleSentences.english: I want to become a translator.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のともだちはほんやくかで、えいごと日本ごとスペインごができます。
exampleSentences.english: My friend is a translator and can speak English, Japanese, and Spanish.

exampleSentences.japanese: 日本ごをほんやくします。
exampleSentences.english: I translate Japanese.

exampleSentences.japanese: このドキュメントをえいごにほんやくしてください。
exampleSentences.english: Please translate this document into English.

exampleSentences.japanese: しごとで日本のしょうせつをえいごにほんやくしていますから、まいにちいそがしいです。
exampleSentences.english: I translate Japanese novels into English for work, so I'm busy every day.

exampleSentences.japanese: じしょをつかいます。
exampleSentences.english: I use a dictionary.

exampleSentences.japanese: わからないことばはじしょで見ます。
exampleSentences.english: I look up words I don't understand in the dictionary.

exampleSentences.japanese: 日本ごをべんきょうするとき、いつもスマホのじしょをつかっています。
exampleSentences.english: When I study Japanese, I always use a dictionary on my smartphone.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のかいしゃは東京にあります。
exampleSentences.english: My company is in Tokyo.

exampleSentences.japanese: このかいしゃはとても大きいです。
exampleSentences.english: This company is very big.

exampleSentences.japanese: 大学をそつぎょうしてから、大きいかいしゃではたらきたいですが、まずえいごをべんきょうします。
exampleSentences.english: After graduating from university, I want to work at a big company, but first I'll study English.

exampleSentences.japanese: 新しいプロジェクトがあります。
exampleSentences.english: I have a new project.

exampleSentences.japanese: このプロジェクトはたいへんですが、おもしろいです。
exampleSentences.english: This project is tough, but interesting.

exampleSentences.japanese: 来月、大きいプロジェクトがはじまりますから、チームでたくさんミーティングをしています。
exampleSentences.english: A big project starts next month, so we're having many meetings as a team.

exampleSentences.japanese: しめきりはいつですか。
exampleSentences.english: When is the deadline?

exampleSentences.japanese: しめきりは来週の金曜日です。
exampleSentences.english: The deadline is next Friday.

exampleSentences.japanese: しめきりがもうすぐですから、まいにちおそくまではたらいています。
exampleSentences.english: The deadline is coming soon, so I'm working late every day.

exampleSentences.japanese: 今日は何曜日ですか。
exampleSentences.english: What day is it today?

exampleSentences.japanese: しあいは何曜日ですか。土曜日です。
exampleSentences.english: What day is the game? It's Saturday.

exampleSentences.japanese: 日本ごのじゅぎょうは何曜日にありますか。月曜日と水曜日にあります。
exampleSentences.english: What days do you have Japanese class? I have it on Monday and Wednesday.

exampleSentences.japanese: スミスさんはせんせいです。
exampleSentences.english: Mr. Smith is a teacher.

exampleSentences.japanese: スミスさんはアメリカからきました。
exampleSentences.english: Mr. Smith came from America.

exampleSentences.japanese: スミスさんは日本ごがじょうずですから、いつも日本ごで話します。
exampleSentences.english: Mr. Smith is good at Japanese, so he always speaks in Japanese.

exampleSentences.japanese: 山田さんはがくせいです。
exampleSentences.english: Mr. Yamada is a student.

exampleSentences.japanese: 山田さんは私のどうりょうです。
exampleSentences.english: Mr. Yamada is my coworker.

exampleSentences.japanese: 山田さんはとてもしんせつですから、新しいしごとをよくおしえてくれます。
exampleSentences.english: Mr. Yamada is very kind, so he often teaches me new work.

exampleSentences.japanese: スペインごをべんきょうしています。
exampleSentences.english: I'm studying Spanish.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はスペインごがすこしできます。
exampleSentences.english: I can speak a little Spanish.

exampleSentences.japanese: 来年スペインにりょこうしたいですから、今スペインごをべんきょうしています。
exampleSentences.english: I want to travel to Spain next year, so I'm studying Spanish now.

exampleSentences.japanese: きょうしつはどこですか。
exampleSentences.english: Where is the classroom?

exampleSentences.japanese: 日本ごのきょうしつは二階にあります。
exampleSentences.english: The Japanese classroom is on the second floor.

exampleSentences.japanese: このきょうしつはひろくてあかるいですから、べんきょうしやすいです。
exampleSentences.english: This classroom is spacious and bright, so it's easy to study.

exampleSentences.japanese: このスープはしょっぱいです。
exampleSentences.english: This soup is salty.

exampleSentences.japanese: しょっぱい食べ物はあまりすきじゃないです。
exampleSentences.english: I don't really like salty food.

exampleSentences.japanese: このラーメンはすこししょっぱいですから、水をたくさん飲みます。
exampleSentences.english: This ramen is a bit salty, so I drink a lot of water.

exampleSentences.japanese: コーラをください。
exampleSentences.english: Cola, please.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はコーラがすきですが、あまり飲みません。
exampleSentences.english: I like cola, but I don't drink it much.

exampleSentences.japanese: えいがかんでポップコーンとつめたいコーラを買いませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to buy popcorn and cold cola at the movie theater?

updatedWords.english: (I) won't drink, don't take, (I) do not drink

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はコーヒーを飲みません。
exampleSentences.english: I don't drink coffee.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はビールをあまり飲みません。
exampleSentences.english: I don't drink beer much.

exampleSentences.japanese: 明日うんてんしますから、今夜はおさけを飲みません。
exampleSentences.english: I'm driving tomorrow, so I won't drink alcohol tonight.

exampleSentences.japanese: とんかつがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like pork cutlet.

exampleSentences.japanese: ひるごはんにとんかつを食べませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to eat pork cutlet for lunch?

exampleSentences.japanese: 日本のとんかつはとてもおいしいですから、ソースをつけて食べてください。
exampleSentences.english: Japanese pork cutlet is very delicious, so please eat it with sauce.

exampleSentences.japanese: しおをください。
exampleSentences.english: Salt, please.

exampleSentences.japanese: りょうりにしおをいれてください。
exampleSentences.english: Please put salt in the dish.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はステーキにしおとこしょうだけをつかいますが、ソースはつかいません。
exampleSentences.english: I only use salt and pepper on steak, but I don't use sauce.

exampleSentences.japanese: ソースをください。
exampleSentences.english: Sauce, please.

exampleSentences.japanese: とんかつにソースをかけますか。
exampleSentences.english: Do you put sauce on pork cutlet?

exampleSentences.japanese: このソースはからいですが、とてもおいしいですから、ぜひつかってみてください。
exampleSentences.english: This sauce is spicy, but it's very delicious, so please try using it.

exampleSentences.japanese: これをつかいましょう。
exampleSentences.english: Let's use this.

exampleSentences.japanese: 新しいパソコンをつかいましょう。
exampleSentences.english: Let's use the new computer.

exampleSentences.japanese: 時間がありませんから、タクシーをつかいましょう。
exampleSentences.english: We don't have time, so let's use a taxi.

exampleSentences.japanese: なまの魚を食べますか。
exampleSentences.english: Do you eat raw fish?

exampleSentences.japanese: すしはなまの魚をつかいます。
exampleSentences.english: Sushi uses raw fish.

exampleSentences.japanese: 日本りょうりにはなまの魚をつかうりょうりがおおいですから、新しいものがだいじです。
exampleSentences.english: Japanese cuisine has many dishes using raw fish, so freshness is important.

exampleSentences.japanese: とうふがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like tofu.

exampleSentences.japanese: みそしるにとうふをいれます。
exampleSentences.english: I put tofu in miso soup.

exampleSentences.japanese: とうふは体にいいですから、私はまいにち食べています。
exampleSentences.english: Tofu is good for the body, so I eat it every day.
